Specimen information

Test01-SEP-2015Specimen

Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hyperkalemia, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, glucosuria, ketonuria, proteinuria). Liver and electrolyte parameters are mostly within normal limits. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with nephrotic-range proteinuria and poor urine concentration. Recommend clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation.
